nativeRange.habitat: Rheophyte on rocks and stones along fast-flowing rainforest streams, rivers, and waterfalls

decorPreferences: attach rhizome to rocks or driftwood with thread or glue
do NOT bury the rhizome — keep it above substrate
moderate to strong water movement to mimic rheophytic habitat
soft to moderately hard, slightly acidic to neutral water

summary: Schismatoglottis (Schismatoglottis roseospatha) is a slow-growing rheophytic aroid from Borneo, closely related to Bucephalandra. It attaches to rocks or driftwood rather than rooting into substrate, and tolerates a range of lighting conditions, making it suitable for nano tanks and midground placements.
careNotes: Schismatoglottis roseospatha is a recently introduced aroid from Sarawak, Borneo, found growing as a rheophyte on rocks and roots along fast-flowing rainforest streams. In the aquarium it is treated much like Bucephalandra or Anubias: the creeping rhizome is attached to hardscape (rock or driftwood) with thread or cyanoacrylate glue; the rhizome must never be buried, as covered rhizomes tend to rot. Submerged it stays compact, generally around 5–15 cm tall, with narrow, pointed bright-green leaves on slim brown to yellowish stems; under good lighting some growers report faint shimmering dots and a bluish vein-pattern through the leaf center. Lighting tolerance is broad (low to high reported); medium light with good flow and modest nutrient/CO2 dosing tends to give the steadiest growth. Like Bucephalandra, it is sensitive to sudden parameter changes during acclimation and may shed a generation of leaves ("melt") before pushing fresh submerged growth — patience over weeks is normal. Propagate by dividing the rhizome with sharp scissors and reattaching the pieces; lateral daughter shoots also form over time. Emersed (in a paludarium or sealed terrarium) it can grow taller, up to roughly 24 cm leaf stalks, and occasionally produces a pink-tinged spathe. Note that several Schismatoglottis species are now in the aquarium trade (e.g., S. prietoi); this entry covers S. roseospatha specifically.

additional.name: Aquasabi
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (22–26°C), pH (4–7), KH (0–14), GH (0–30), lighting (low), growth rate (very slow), submerged height (<8 cm), Sarawak origin, Araceae family, epiphytic placement.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Third source for cross-verification. Confirms temperature 22–26°C, pH 4.5–7, KH 0–14, family Araceae, order Alismatales, native habitat Gaad River and Sungai Lelang (Sarawak, Borneo), rheophyte habit, very slow growth. Source swap: original primary Tropica does not carry this species; promoted Buce Plant to primary and added Flowgrow as third source.
